metadata.summary: 1-2M token context, native multimodal input, Live API for real-time voice. Best for long documents, video analysis, and real-time voice agents.

content.title: Gemini API Integration
content.summary: Google Gemini API for multimodal AI applications. Models: Gemini 3 Pro/Flash (1M+ context, dynamic thinking), Gemini 2.0 Flash (fast, agentic), Gemini 1.5 Pro (2M context). Key differentiators: native video/audio input, Live API for real-time voice/video, code execution sandbox, Google Search grounding, context caching (75% cost reduction).

why.p: Gemini is the only LLM provider with a 2M-token context window, native video input, and a Live API for sub-200ms real-time voice. For long-document processing, video analysis, or real-time voice agents, Gemini 1.5 Pro or 2.0 Flash have no direct OpenAI/Claude equivalent.

list.item: Processing documents, codebases, or video that exceeds 128K tokens (use Gemini 1.5 Pro at 2M)
Real-time voice/video applications requiring low latency (use Live API with Gemini 2.0 Flash)
Applications needing code execution in a sandboxed Python environment
Grounding responses in live Google Search results (use grounding tools)
High-volume production with cost sensitivity (Gemini 2.0 Flash at $0.10/$0.40 per 1M tokens)
Agent tooling accuracy is critical: OpenAI and Claude have more mature tool-use ecosystems
Need the strongest available reasoning: use Claude Opus or o3 for frontier reasoning tasks
Enterprise compliance requiring no Google data processing — use Azure OpenAI or Claude on AWS

section.name: model-lineup
section.p: Gemini model lineup as of January 2026 with context windows, capabilities, and recommended use cases.

list.item: Gemini 3 Pro — 1M+ context, highest intelligence, dynamic thinking; use for complex reasoning and agents
Gemini 3 Flash — 1M+ context, fast + smart, dynamic thinking; use for production apps
Gemini 2.0 Flash — 1M context, fast, multimodal, agentic; use for real-time and agentic apps
Gemini 1.5 Pro — 2M context (largest), balanced; use for long documents and codebases
Gemini 1.5 Flash — 1M context, cost-effective; use for high-volume production
Gemini 1.5 Flash-8B — smallest, cheapest; use for simple classification tasks

rule.statement: Default to Gemini 3 Flash for new production apps; escalate to Gemini 3 Pro only when benchmark quality matters.
rule.rationale: Gemini 3 Flash is fast, supports dynamic thinking, and costs the same as 2.0 Flash ($0.10/$0.40 per 1M tokens). Pro adds latency and cost with marginal quality gains on most tasks.

section.name: gemini-3-features
section.p: Gemini 3 introduces dynamic thinking, thought signatures, multimodal function responses, and media resolution control.

list.item: Dynamic thinking: auto-adjusts reasoning depth; levels are low/medium/high/minimal; default high for Pro, medium for Flash
Thought signatures: encrypted reasoning tokens required even at minimal thinking level in multi-turn conversations
Multimodal function responses: tools can return images, videos, PDFs alongside text
Media resolution control: low/medium/high/ultra-high per image/frame; affects token cost

rule.statement: For Gemini 3: use simpler prompts and temperature 1.0 (default) — do not set thinking_budget manually unless profiling shows it is needed.
rule.rationale: Gemini 3 reasoning is adaptive. Over-specified prompts and low temperature fight the model's built-in planning. Let thinking_level=high handle complex tasks; only override when cost/latency is a hard constraint.

section.name: multimodal-inputs
section.p: Supported media types, upload methods, and size constraints for multimodal input processing.

list.item: Images: PIL.Image or base64; supported: JPEG, PNG, WebP, HEIC, HEIF
Video: upload via genai.upload_file(), poll for ACTIVE state before use; max 1 hour
Audio: upload or inline; 96 languages; timestamps available
PDFs: upload as file or pass bytes; multi-page fully supported
Large files (>20MB): use File API upload, not inline bytes

section.name: context-caching
section.p: Context caching stores fixed prompt prefixes on Google infrastructure, reducing input cost by 75% for reused contexts.

rule.statement: Use context caching for any prompt prefix >32K tokens that is reused across requests — it cuts input cost by 75%.
rule.rationale: Context caching stores a fixed prefix (system prompt + large document) on Google's infrastructure. Subsequent calls only pay for the incremental tokens. At 1M-token contexts, this transforms cost economics.

list.item: Minimum cache size: 32K tokens; smaller prefixes do not qualify
Cache TTL: configurable, default 1 hour; billed at ~$0.3125/1M tokens storage (Gemini 1.5 Pro)
Best for: multi-turn Q&A over the same large document, repeated system prompt + large context
Gemini 3 Pro: $1.25 input / $5.00 output per 1M tokens
Gemini 3 Flash: $0.10 input / $0.40 output per 1M tokens
Gemini 2.0 Flash: $0.10 input / $0.40 output per 1M tokens
Free tier: Gemini 2.0 Flash 10 RPM / 1,000 RPD; Gemini 1.5 Flash 15 RPM / 1,500 RPD

section.name: chat-and-streaming
section.p: Chat and streaming integration patterns for multi-turn conversations and real-time token delivery.

rule.statement: Always use start_chat() for multi-turn conversations — never manually append history to generate_content().
rule.rationale: start_chat() manages history state automatically including thought signatures (Gemini 3 requirement). Manual history management misses thought tokens and breaks multi-turn coherence.

list.item: Streaming: use stream=True in send_message(); yields chunk.text as tokens arrive; improves perceived latency
Session management: store chat objects per session_id; use Redis in production (not in-memory dict)
Reset: call model.start_chat() to create a fresh session; no in-place reset method

section.name: function-calling
section.p: Function calling with automatic schema generation from Python type hints and docstrings.

rule.statement: Pass Python functions directly as tools — Gemini SDK auto-generates the schema from type hints and docstrings.
rule.rationale: Auto-schema generation eliminates manual JSON Schema maintenance. Docstring Args: sections become parameter descriptions. This is Gemini's native pattern and is more reliable than manual FunctionDeclaration objects.

list.item: Use enable_automatic_function_calling=True for simple agents; handle manually for human-in-the-loop flows
Tool choice: configure tool_config with mode=ANY to force tool use, NONE to disable
Parallel calls: Gemini returns multiple function_call parts; execute all before sending results back

section.name: rag-with-gemini
section.p: RAG patterns using Gemini's embedding model and long-context window capabilities.

list.item: Embedding model: models/text-embedding-004; 768 dimensions; superior to ada-002 on MTEB
Task types: RETRIEVAL_DOCUMENT for indexing, RETRIEVAL_QUERY for queries — always specify
Batch embed: pass a list to genai.embed_content() for efficiency; do not embed one-by-one in loops
For long-context RAG: pass all chunks directly at 1.5 Pro 2M context; skip vector DB for <500 docs

section.name: security
section.p: Security checklist for API key management, safety filtering, and compliance.

list.item: Never expose GOOGLE_API_KEY in client-side code; use server-side proxy for browser apps
Check response.prompt_feedback.block_reason before accessing response.text; blocked responses raise AttributeError
Validate safety_ratings on every response; HARM_BLOCK_THRESHOLD configurable per category
For enterprise compliance, use Vertex AI (not Google AI Studio API); data stays in your GCP project

antipattern.description: Accessing response.text when the response was safety-blocked.
antipattern.reason: A blocked response has no .text attribute; accessing it raises AttributeError. Always check response.prompt_feedback.block_reason is None before using response.text.

antipattern.description: Using Gemini 3 with low temperature (e.g., 0.1) for structured extraction.
antipattern.reason: Gemini 3's dynamic thinking works best at default temperature 1.0. Low temperature suppresses reasoning. For structured output use response_mime_type="application/json" with response_schema instead of lowering temperature.

antipattern.description: Uploading the same large file on every request instead of caching.
antipattern.reason: File upload adds latency and does not benefit from context caching. Upload once, store the genai.File reference, reuse across sessions. Files are retained for 48 hours by default.

antipattern.description: Using Gemini for tool-heavy agents without fallback to OpenAI.
antipattern.reason: Gemini function calling accuracy lags OpenAI GPT-4o on dense tool catalogues. For production agents with >10 tools, benchmark both providers before committing to Gemini.

example.title: Chat with Streaming
Function Calling with Auto-Schema
Context Caching for Large Documents
example.code:
# Upload large document once response = genai.upload_file(path="large_codebase.pdf") file = response
# Use with context caching model = genai.GenerativeModel("gemini-1-5-pro") contents = [ genai.protos.Content( role="user", parts=[ genai.protos.Part.from_uri(file_uri=file.uri, mime_type=file.mime_type), genai.protos.Part(text="Summarize the key patterns in this codebase") ] ) ]
# Subsequent queries reuse the cached prefix for query in ["Find security issues", "Explain the architecture"]: contents.append(genai.protos.Content(role="user", parts=[genai.protos.Part(text=query)])) response = model.generate_content(contents) print(f"Query: {query}\nResponse: {response.text}\n")

template.purpose: Production Gemini client: model setup, retry logic, streaming, function calling, chat session
Image analyzer + video analyzer + document processor classes
